qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Quadratic-time DoS in strip_tags() via Python HTMLParser unclosed-comment parsing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns django.utils.html.strip_tags() and Python HTMLParser quadratic parsing of unclosed comments, not PostGIS RasterField raster lookup band-index SQL construction.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Object-level permission bypass in Django admin enables IDOR attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns Django admin ModelAdmin object permission behavior, not RasterField lookups or unvalidated raster band indices in PostGIS SQL generation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Static file serving without authorization allows unauthorized access to sensitive files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns django.views.static.serve() serving files without authorization, not PostGIS RasterField raster lookup band-index SQL injection.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory root cause: CVE-2026-1207 is SQL injection in Django PostGIS RasterField raster lookups when an untrusted band index reaches check_raster() without integer validation. The qualifying findings instead cover strip_tags() HTML parsing DoS, Django admin object-level permission assumptions, and static file serving authorization behavior.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mwm9-4648-f68q describes RasterField lookups on PostGIS allowing SQL injection via the band index parameter.
Advisory patch 81aa5292967cd09319c45fe2c1a525ce7b6684d8 adds integer type checks for lookup.band_lhs and lookup.band_rhs in django/contrib/gis/db/backends/postgis/operations.py::check_raster().

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_1eb8eb77/.judge/input_manifest.json lists exactly three qualifying final findings: strip_tags_dos, vuln-idor-admin-permissions, and vuln-static-file-authorization-bypass.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_1eb8eb77/no-vuln-sql-injection/vulnerability_report.json reports no qualifying SQL injection finding and does not identify RasterField, PostGIS, band_lhs, band_rhs, or check_raster().
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
